title: pouteria firma
intro: Pouteria firma, also known as the white wrightia or white wood, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. Its leaves and roots have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing. Pouteria firma is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to alleviate various health conditions.
